document.diagnosis: Iteration 29 was selection-limited. The nine-bank selector exposed correct candidates on 6/12 cases but converted only 2/12, including 0/4 constraint accuracy despite constraint oracle coverage. Cross-examination led at 4/12 by converting four of five expanded-oracle cases, making three helpful and zero harmful interventions, and creating one correct logic answer absent from the base bank. No strategy exceeded 25% weakest-family accuracy. The established five-bank three-review champion is now 140/336 exact (41.7%) through Iteration 29; the nine-bank one-selector reached the five-panel threshold at 27/66 (40.9%) but its latest-panel weakest-family accuracy was 0%, so it has not displaced the incumbent. Iteration 30 should retain both the champion and current cross-examination winner, replicate the candidate-only selector at checkpoint scale, and preserve the matched one-review efficiency ablation.
extensions_requested: Add a shared-evidence-board primitive: one domain-neutral critic records candidate-by-candidate decisive claims, contradictions, checks, and unresolved issues without choosing; one subsequent selector sees the shuffled candidates and board and returns an exact answer. This directly tests whether explicit evidence externalization improves oracle conversion.
most_important_evidence: Iteration 29 nine-bank selection: 6/12 base-oracle coverage but only 2/12 exact, leaving four covered cases unconverted.
Cross-examination improved the common five-bank plurality from 1/12 to 4/12 through three helpful interventions, zero harmful reversals, and one newly created correct logic answer.
The pooled champion improved the same 1/12 base plurality to 3/12 with two helpful and zero harmful interventions; its long-run record is 140/336 exact.
The nine-bank one-selector is 27/66 exact after five panels, but fell to 2/12 with 0% constraint accuracy in Iteration 29.
Five-bank one-review repair used two fewer calls than the champion and still produced two helpful and zero harmful interventions, although its current-panel weakest-family accuracy was 0%.

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the minimum-call independent baseline unchanged for checkpoint calibration.
proposals.name: Direct One

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the deterministic five-answer plurality control unchanged to measure the value added by every review architecture.
proposals.name: Five-Vote Plurality

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the established pooled champion unchanged; its large-sample record and positive correction balance remain the strongest incumbent evidence.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the Iteration 29 winner unchanged to test whether sequential falsification reliably reproduces its superior conversion of a shared five-answer bank.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the exact five-panel candidate-only selector at checkpoint scale. Its larger bank supplies materially higher oracle coverage, while its recent failure directly tests whether a single selector can convert that coverage reliably.
proposals.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

proposals.hypothesis: Continue the matched call-efficiency comparison against the champion: one answer-generating falsifier costs two fewer calls per case and retains a positive helpful-minus-harmful intervention balance.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

research_hypotheses: Sequential cross-examination may improve conversion by carrying forward a falsifiable proposal and critique rather than aggregating independent repair outputs.
The nine-bank selector's main uncertainty is candidate conversion, not candidate availability; an 18-case replication will distinguish its earlier balanced panels from the Iteration 29 collapse.
If one-review repair remains close to three-review repair, the two additional reviewers are not justified by their call cost.
An explicit evidence board may address the persistent gap between oracle coverage and final accuracy more directly than adding candidates or selector votes.
document.risk_note: The complete six-strategy batch has a worst-case cost of 306 Luna calls: 162 shared bank calls from the largest base_count of nine, plus 144 review calls from review depths 3+3+1+1 across 18 cases. This is safely below the 500-call cap. The main statistical risk is fresh-panel variance; crossing the five-panel threshold permits comparison but does not itself establish superiority.
